name: Payload CMS MCP Server
what_it_does: Payload CMS MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Payload CMS — the modern TypeScript-native headless CMS — creating and updating content collections, querying documents, managing media, and integrating Payload's content management capabilities into agent-driven content creation and publishing workflows.
use_cases: Creating and updating CMS content from content generation agents
Querying content collections for information retrieval from knowledge agents
Publishing and scheduling content from editorial workflow agents
Managing media and assets from content pipeline agents
Automating content migration and transformation from DevOps agents
Building content-aware RAG pipelines from knowledge management agents
not_for: Teams not using Payload CMS (use WordPress, Contentful, or Strapi MCPs)
Teams without a running Payload CMS instance
best_when: A developer team uses Payload CMS and wants AI agents to create, update, and query content — automating editorial workflows and content operations on their Payload instance.
avoid_when: You use WordPress, Contentful, Strapi, or another CMS — use the appropriate MCP.

auth.notes: Payload CMS API key or JWT token required. Configure Payload to issue API keys. Local or cloud Payload instance URL must be set.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Payload CMS is completely free and open source
pricing.paid_tiers: Payload Cloud: paid hosting; self-hosted is free
pricing.notes: Payload CMS is free open source. Community MCP is free. Cloud hosting costs apply if using Payload Cloud.

performance.rate_limits: Depends on hosting; self-hosted has no external limits

known_agent_gotchas: Payload CMS must be running and accessible from MCP server
Collection schemas vary by implementation — agents need schema context
Payload v2 vs v3 API differences — verify MCP compatibility with your version
Community MCP from ai-primitives — moderate maintenance
Content write operations need careful validation before publishing
GraphQL and REST APIs both available — MCP may use either
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS recommended. API key or JWT. Community MCP. Content management is privileged — use read-only for monitoring.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS. API key/JWT. Read-only for monitoring. Community MCP.
